trigger: Volta read-only review found that tracked TT5L custody artifacts could lag current no-op hardening and that the side-info effect-curve dispatch planner did not require the new no-op proof fields.

changes.summary: Dispatch planner now fails closed when variant rows omit generation_rule, variant_seed, source archive/member/side-section SHA-256 fields, or the source-change booleans needed to prove no-op/non-no-op behavior.
Added fixture custody fields and a regression test that removes side-section custody fields from random_lsb and expects ready_for_operator_dispatch=false.
Regenerated the legacy all-zero source variant manifest with the current builder so historical random_lsb/all-zero controls carry the same source-change/no-op custody fields.
Rebuilt the current five-variant effect-curve dispatch plan under the stricter custody-field validator.

remaining_blockers: No full 600-pair trained TT5L archive with proven useful nonzero side-info has been exact-evaluated.
TT5L side-info effect claims still require paired contest-CPU and contest-CUDA cells for all five current archive SHA-256 values.
Modal remains blocked by workspace billing limits; Lightning current plan is CUDA dry-run only and needs identity/teamspace/source-staging/remote-CUDA preflights before non-dry-run.
